front: What is the Iceberg REST Catalog specification, and why is it becoming the standard?
back: The Iceberg REST Catalog is an HTTP-based API specification (defined in the Iceberg open-source project) for catalog operations: list namespaces, list tables, load table metadata, create tables, commit transactions. Any service implementing this spec can serve as an Iceberg catalog. It decouples Iceberg clients from specific catalog implementations — your compute (Spark, Flink, Trino) only needs to speak the REST API, and the catalog backend can be anything: Project Nessie, Polaris, Tabular, Unity Catalog, AWS S3Tables, or a custom implementation. This is the 'ODBC moment' for table formats: a standard interface that enables multi-engine, multi-catalog interoperability. Major implementations: Project Nessie (git-like branching for data), Apache Polaris (Snowflake-donated, open-source), Tabular (now part of Databricks Unity Catalog), AWS S3Tables (GA 2024). Production recommendation: deploy a REST catalog for any multi-engine lakehouse — avoids vendor lock-in and enables mixing compute engines without reconfiguration.
